title: Add CI workflow for pull request validation
body:
Add a GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs on every pull request and push to `main` to validate all three services (`apps/launcher`, `apps/admin`, `services/registry-api`).

## Scope

The workflow should:
1. Install dependencies (`npm ci`) for each service.
2. Run TypeScript type-checking (`tsc --noEmit` or the existing `build` script).
3. Run the `npm test` script for each service.
4. Use a matrix strategy to run all three services in parallel.
5. Cache `node_modules` using `actions/cache` or `actions/setup-node` caching to speed up runs.
6. Target Node.js 20 (matching the Dockerfiles).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `.github/workflows/ci.yml` exists and triggers on `pull_request` and `push` to `main`
- [ ] All three services (`apps/launcher`, `apps/admin`, `services/registry-api`) are built and tested
- [ ] Jobs run in parallel via a matrix strategy
- [ ] Node.js dependency caching is enabled
- [ ] Workflow passes on a clean checkout of `main`

title: Add ESLint configuration and lint CI step
body:
The project currently has no linting configured. Add ESLint to all three services and integrate linting into the CI pipeline.

## Scope

1. Add ESLint with TypeScript support to each service:
   - `apps/launcher` — React + TypeScript rules
   - `apps/admin` — React + TypeScript rules
   - `services/registry-api` — Node.js + TypeScript rules
2. Add a `lint` script to each `package.json`.
3. Add a lint step to the CI workflow (from the PR validation workflow issue).
4. Fix any lint errors in existing code or add targeted rule overrides where appropriate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ESLint config exists for each service
- [ ] `npm run lint` works in each service directory
- [ ] CI workflow includes a lint step that fails the build on lint errors
- [ ] Existing code passes lint (or has documented, intentional overrides)

title: Add unit tests to replace placeholder test scripts
body:
All three services currently have placeholder test scripts (`echo "Tests passed" && exit 0`). Add a real test framework and initial unit tests so that CI validates actual behavior.

## Scope

### `services/registry-api`
- Add a test framework (e.g., Vitest or Jest with ts-jest).
- Unit tests for:
  - Demo store CRUD operations (InMemoryStore)
  - URL validation logic
  - API route handlers (supertest or similar)

### `apps/launcher`
- Add Vitest (pairs well with Vite).
- Unit tests for:
  - Key UI state transitions (idle timeout, demo launch/exit)
  - Demo tile rendering given registry data

### `apps/admin`
- Add Vitest.
- Unit tests for:
  - Form validation (demo create/edit)
  - Registry data fetching and display

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each service has a real test framework installed and configured
- [ ] `npm test` runs actual tests (not echo placeholders)
- [ ] At least 3 meaningful unit tests per service
- [ ] Tests do not depend on external demo apps being reachable (mock external calls)
- [ ] All tests pass in CI

title: Add Docker image build validation to CI
body:
Add a CI workflow (or job in the existing CI workflow) that builds the Docker images for all three services on every pull request to catch Dockerfile and build issues early.

## Scope

1. Build Docker images for `apps/launcher`, `apps/admin`, and `services/registry-api` using their respective Dockerfiles.
2. Use `docker build` (no push) to validate the build succeeds.
3. Optionally use Docker layer caching (e.g., `docker/build-push-action` with cache) to speed up builds.
4. Run on `pull_request` events.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Docker images for all three services build successfully in CI on every PR
- [ ] Build failures in any Dockerfile fail the CI check
- [ ] Docker layer caching is configured to minimize build times
- [ ] No images are pushed to a registry (build-only validation)

title: Add container image publish workflow
body:
Add a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and pushes Docker images to a container registry when changes are merged to `main`. This is a prerequisite for automated deployment to Azure Container Apps.

## Scope

1. Trigger on `push` to `main` (or on release tags).
2. Build Docker images for all three services.
3. Push images to a container registry (GitHub Container Registry `ghcr.io` recommended).
4. Tag images with the Git SHA and `latest`.
5. Use GitHub Actions OIDC or `GITHUB_TOKEN` for authentication to `ghcr.io`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Workflow triggers on merge to `main`
- [ ] Docker images for all three services are built and pushed to `ghcr.io`
- [ ] Images are tagged with the Git commit SHA and `latest`
- [ ] Authentication uses `GITHUB_TOKEN` (no manual secrets needed for ghcr.io)
- [ ] Workflow does not run on pull requests (build-only validation is separate)

title: Add CD workflow for Azure Container Apps deployment
body:
Add a GitHub Actions workflow that deploys the published container images to Azure Container Apps (ACA) after a successful image publish to the container registry.

## Scope

1. Trigger after the container image publish workflow completes successfully (use `workflow_run` or combine into a single workflow).
2. Deploy all three services to ACA:
   - `registry-api` — backend API
   - `launcher` — frontend SPA
   - `admin` — admin frontend SPA
3. Use Azure CLI (`az containerapp update`) or the `azure/container-apps-deploy-action` GitHub Action.
4. Configure authentication via Azure OIDC federated credentials (preferred) or repository secrets for Azure service principal.
5. Support environment-based configuration (e.g., staging vs production) via workflow inputs or branch-based logic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Deployment workflow exists and triggers after successful image publish
- [ ] All three services are deployed to Azure Container Apps
- [ ] Azure authentication uses OIDC federated credentials (or documented service principal secrets)
- [ ] Deployment is idempotent (re-running produces the same result)
- [ ] Health probe endpoints are configured in ACA for each service
- [ ] Workflow logs clearly indicate which revision was deployed
